Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Look up Derek Hollis's contact details and company using crm_search_contacts (query: "Derek Hollis") or contacts_search_contacts (query: "Derek Hollis") to confirm his email and role at GreenStack Energy.
Search emails for the GreenStack relocation request using email_search_emails (query: "Noah Fitzgerald relocation Seattle") to find the original request from Derek Hollis and understand the scope of the move.
Search emails for the flight booking request using email_search_emails (query: "flight booking Noah Fitzgerald" or "Atlas Corporate Travel") to find Fatimah's booking request to Atlas specifying passenger Noah Fitzgerald, client GreenStack Energy, PHL to SEA.
Search emails for the Atlas flight confirmation using email_search_emails (query: "ACT-77492" or "AS 422" or "Alaska Airlines") to find the confirmed itinerary: confirmation ACT-77492, Alaska Airlines AS 422, April 14, PHL to SEA.
Search emails sent to Noah Fitzpatrick (Axiom) using email_search_emails (query: "n.fitzpatrick@axiomprecision.com" or "Noah Fitzpatrick itinerary") to discover the flight confirmation was sent to the wrong person, the Axiom employee instead of the GreenStack employee.
Search emails sent to Noah Fitzgerald (GreenStack) using email_search_emails (query: "noah.fitzgerald@greenstackenergy.com flight" or "Noah Fitzgerald confirmation") to verify that GreenStack's employee never received his flight itinerary.
Search Slack messages using slack_search_messages (query: "Fitzpatrick" or "Fitzgerald" or "two Noahs") to find the operations channel exchange where Fatimah asked Emeka to clarify the names and Emeka confirmed Fitzpatrick equals Axiom, Fitzgerald equals GreenStack.
Search Linear issues for GreenStack flight tracking using linear_search_issues (query: "Fitzgerald flight" or "GreenStack Seattle") to find the GreenStack flight booking issue showing the intended flight date was April 27, not April 14.
Search Linear issues or emails for Noah Fitzgerald's intended travel date using linear_search_issues (query: "Fitzgerald" or "GreenStack") or email_search_emails (query: "Noah Fitzgerald preferences") to confirm that his requested flight date was April 27.
Look up the CEO's contact info using contacts_search_contacts (query: "CEO" or "Elena Rostova") to find elena.rostova@moveops.com.
Look up the customer support lead's contact info using contacts_search_contacts (query: "customer support" or "Julian Brooks") to find julian.brooks@moveops.com, Lead Customer Support Specialist.
Send email to Derek Hollis at derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com using email_send_email with a full explanation of what happened: the flight booked for Noah Fitzgerald (AS 422, confirmation ACT-77492, April 14 PHL to SEA) was mistakenly sent to a different client's employee with a similar name. Include the correct booking details, the intended April 27 date, and next steps. CC elena.rostova@moveops.com (CEO) and julian.brooks@moveops.com (customer support lead).

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The model must use a contact lookup tool (contacts_search_contacts or crm_search_contacts) to search for contact details.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent used both contacts_search_contacts (with queries 'Derek', 'CEO', 'customer support', 'Elena', 'Julian Brooks', 'Mina', 'Marcus Thorne', 'David Chen') and crm_search_contacts (with full_name 'Derek'). These calls successfully retrieved Derek Hollis's email (derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com), Elena Rostova as CEO (elena.rostova@moveops.com), and Julian Brooks was found via contacts_get_contacts (julian.brooks@moveops.com, Lead Customer Support Specialist).
✓Run #2The agent used contacts_search_contacts multiple times (queries: 'Derek', 'CEO', 'David Chen', 'Marcus', 'Mina', 'Elena', 'Catalina') and crm_search_contacts with full_name 'Derek Hollis'. These calls successfully retrieved Derek Hollis's email (derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com), Elena Rostova as CEO (elena.rostova@moveops.com), and Julian Brooks as Lead Customer Support Specialist (julian.brooks@moveops.com) from the contacts list.
✓Run #3The agent used crm_search_contacts with query 'Derek' (finding Derek Hollis at GreenStack Energy) and contacts_search_contacts with 'CEO' and then 'moveops' (which returned Elena Rostova as CEO and Julian Brooks as Lead Customer Support Specialist). Both tools were invoked to look up contact details.
✓Run #4The agent used both `crm_search_contacts` (with query 'Derek') and `contacts_search_contacts` (with queries 'Derek', 'Elena', 'Emeka', 'support', etc.). The CRM search found Derek Hollis at greenstackenergy.com. The contacts search found Elena Rostova (CEO). The full contacts list (`contacts_get_contacts`) was also retrieved, which included Julian Brooks (Lead Customer Support Specialist).
✓Run #5The agent used both `crm_search_contacts` (query: 'Derek') and `contacts_search_contacts` (queries: 'Derek', 'Elena', 'CEO', 'support', 'Marcus', 'Mina', 'Catalina', 'Chloe') to look up contact details. These calls returned Derek Hollis at derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com, Elena Rostova (CEO) at elena.rostova@moveops.com, and Julian Brooks (Lead Customer Support Specialist) at julian.brooks@moveops.com.
✓Run #6The agent used both crm_search_contacts (querying 'Derek' and 'GreenStack') and contacts_search_contacts (querying 'Derek', 'CEO', 'customer support', 'MoveOps', 'Marcus', 'Elena'). Derek Hollis's email was correctly retrieved, and Elena Rostova (CEO) and Julian Brooks (Lead Customer Support) were found.
2 The model must use an email search tool (email_search_emails or search_emails) to investigate the flight itinerary situation.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent used search_emails with multiple relevant queries including 'GreenStack' (which returned the Atlas Corporate Travel confirmation for Noah Fitzgerald, flight AS 422, ACT-77492), 'Noah Fitzgerald flight itinerary', 'Noah Fitzpatrick', 'Axiom', 'Atlas Corporate Travel' (SENT folder), 'flight booking Seattle', and 'Joaquin Reyes'. The 'GreenStack' search was the key call that surfaced the Atlas confirmation email.
✓Run #2The agent called search_emails multiple times with queries including 'Seattle relocation', 'Seattle flight', 'GreenStack', 'itinerary', 'Noah Fitzpatrick', 'Axiom Precision', 'ACT-77492', and 'Noah Fitzgerald flight'. The query 'GreenStack' returned the Atlas confirmation email with booking details, and 'ACT-77492' returned the specific flight confirmation from derek.saunders@atlascorporatetravel.com.
✓Run #3The agent called search_emails multiple times with queries including 'GreenStack' (which returned the Atlas confirmation email for Noah Fitzgerald with ACT-77492 and AS 422), 'Atlas Corporate Travel', 'Noah Fitzgerald flight booking', 'Fitzgerald booking request PHL', and several others. The flight booking chain was investigated via email search.
✓Run #4The agent made multiple `search_emails` calls with relevant queries including 'GreenStack', 'itinerary', 'Seattle relocation', 'Noah Fitzpatrick', and 'Noah Fitzgerald' (in SENT folder). The 'GreenStack' email search returned the Atlas Corporate Travel confirmation email with flight AS 422 and confirmation ACT-77492.
✓Run #5The agent called `search_emails` multiple times with queries including 'GreenStack Seattle', 'Seattle relocation', 'flight itinerary', 'Derek Hollis', 'Noah Fitzgerald', 'flight Seattle', 'Fitzgerald itinerary', 'Atlas Corporate Travel Fitzgerald', 'ACT-77492', 'Atlas Corporate Travel' (SENT), and 'Fitzgerald' (SENT). These searches successfully located the flight confirmation email from Atlas Corporate Travel.
✓Run #6The agent called search_emails multiple times with queries including 'Seattle relocation', 'GreenStack', 'Noah Fitzgerald flight', 'Noah Fitzgerald', 'Atlas Corporate Travel', 'Joaquin Reyes', 'PHX SEA', etc. The Atlas Corporate Travel confirmation email (ACT-77492, AS 422) was successfully retrieved via this tool.
3 The model must use a Slack search or history tool (slack_search_messages or conversations_history or similar) to check for prior team awareness of the name confusion.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent used conversations_search_messages with 'GreenStack', which returned the critical Slack exchange where Fatimah asked 'is the Axiom one Fitzpatrick or Fitzgerald?', Emeka confirmed 'Fitzpatrick — the Axiom one. Fitzgerald is GreenStack', and Fatimah acknowledged. The agent also searched Slack with 'Noah Fitzpatrick', 'Noah Fitzgerald flight wrong', 'mix-up Noah', 'itinerary never received', 'CEO', and 'flight itinerary'.
✓Run #2The agent used conversations_search_messages multiple times with queries including 'Seattle relocation', 'GreenStack', 'Seattle flight', 'Noah Fitzgerald flight', and 'Fitzpatrick'. The 'Fitzpatrick' query successfully returned the Slack exchange in the operations channel showing the name confusion between the two Noahs.
✓Run #3The agent used conversations_search_messages (Slack search) multiple times with queries including 'Seattle relocation', 'GreenStack', 'flight itinerary', 'Derek', 'Joaquin Reyes flight', 'Noah Fitzgerald flight', 'Joaquin Reyes', 'Noah Fitzgerald itinerary', and 'wrong flight'. The Slack tool was used to check for prior team awareness.
✓Run #4The agent used `conversations_search_messages` multiple times with queries including 'Seattle', 'GreenStack', 'Noah Fitzpatrick', and 'flight itinerary'. The 'GreenStack' Slack search found Fatimah's message 'Both of his relocations are to Seattle in April' showing she confused the two cases. The 'Noah Fitzpatrick' search found Emeka's warning to Fatimah about the name coincidence.
✓Run #5The agent called `conversations_search_messages` six times with queries: 'Seattle relocation', 'GreenStack', 'flight itinerary', 'Noah Fitzgerald', 'Derek itinerary flight', and 'Fitzgerald flight booking'. This satisfies the requirement to search Slack for prior team awareness.
✓Run #6The agent used conversations_search_messages (with multiple queries) and conversations_history (for channels C006/operations and C002/customer-engagement). Slack was searched for GreenStack, Fitzgerald, Noah Fitzgerald, flight issues, etc.
4 The model must use a Linear tool (linear_search_issues, linear_list_issues, or similar) to check for relocation tracking tickets.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent used linear_list_issues with query 'Fitzgerald', which returned Linear tickets #316 (Apartment Search — Noah Fitzgerald, state: in_progress), #317 (Flight Booking — Noah Fitzgerald, state: todo, target date April 27), and #318 (Moving Coordination — Noah Fitzgerald, state: todo). The agent also queried linear_list_issues with 'Joaquin Reyes', 'Fitzpatrick', and 'Seattle relocation'.
✓Run #2The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times with queries including 'Seattle relocation', 'Seattle', 'GreenStack', and 'Joaquin Reyes flight'. The 'GreenStack' and 'Seattle' queries returned Linear tickets including the Noah Fitzgerald flight booking ticket (issue_greenstack_fitzgerald_flight) with state_todo status and target date April 27.

Linear was searched for relocation tracking tickets.

The 'GreenStack' and 'relocation' searches returned tickets including issue #317 (Flight Booking – Noah Fitzgerald – PHL→SEA, state_todo, target April 27) confirming the flight was never booked.
✓Run #5The agent called `linear_list_issues` twice — once with query 'Seattle' (which returned GreenStack Fitzgerald issues) and once with query 'flight' (which returned issue #317 for Noah Fitzgerald's flight with state 'state_todo' and target date April 27). This clearly satisfies the criterion.
✓Run #6The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times with queries 'Seattle relocation', 'GreenStack', 'Seattle', 'Joaquin Reyes flight', 'Reyes'. The GreenStack Fitzgerald flight ticket (target date April 27) and related relocation issues were retrieved.

6 The model must pass a query related to "Fitzgerald" or "Noah Fitzgerald" or "GreenStack flight" (or similar) when searching for the flight booking chain.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent searched emails with 'GreenStack' (which returned the Atlas confirmation email for 'Noah Fitzgerald (GreenStack Energy) PHL→SEA' with AS 422 and ACT-77492), and also directly with 'Noah Fitzgerald flight itinerary'. The 'GreenStack' query was the one that successfully retrieved the Atlas flight confirmation chain.
✓Run #2The agent searched emails with the query 'GreenStack', which returned multiple emails including the Noah Fitzgerald relocation request from Derek Hollis and the Atlas Corporate Travel confirmation (Re: Flight Booking Request – Noah Fitzgerald (GreenStack Energy) – PHL → SEA). The agent also directly searched 'ACT-77492' to retrieve the confirmation email with booking details.
✓Run #3The agent searched emails with 'Noah Fitzgerald flight booking' and 'Fitzgerald booking request PHL', as well as 'GreenStack' (which returned the Atlas confirmation for Fitzgerald). These are clearly Fitzgerald/GreenStack flight related queries that enabled tracing the booking chain.
✓Run #4The agent searched emails with query 'GreenStack' (which returned the Atlas flight confirmation email for Noah Fitzgerald, including AS 422 and ACT-77492) and also with query 'Noah Fitzgerald' in the SENT folder (which returned Fatimah's flight booking request to Atlas). Both queries are directly related to the flight booking chain.
✓Run #5The agent searched emails with the query 'Noah Fitzgerald' (which returned the Atlas confirmation email, the UrbanNest apartment options, Noah's own email specifying April 27, and the original relocation request) and also searched 'Fitzgerald itinerary' and 'Atlas Corporate Travel Fitzgerald'. These are clearly Fitzgerald/GreenStack flight related queries.
✓Run #6The agent searched emails with the query 'Noah Fitzgerald flight' and also 'Noah Fitzgerald' (folder_name: SENT), directly satisfying the criterion of querying for Fitzgerald or Noah Fitzgerald when investigating the flight booking chain.
7 The model must pass a query related to "Fitzpatrick" or "Fitzgerald" or "two Noahs" (or similar) when searching for the name confusion exchange.
query construction
 ✓✓✗✓✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The agent searched Slack (conversations_search_messages) with 'Noah Fitzpatrick', which directly queries for Fitzpatrick-related messages. The earlier 'GreenStack' Slack search also returned the Fitzpatrick/Fitzgerald exchange (ts 1770104640-1770105420), where Fatimah asked specifically about the Fitzpatrick vs Fitzgerald name distinction.
✓Run #2The agent called conversations_search_messages with query 'Fitzpatrick', which returned the key Slack exchange (tool_call_result_10.json) where Fatimah asked Emeka: 'is the Axiom one 'Fitzpatrick' or 'Fitzgerald'?' and Emeka replied 'Fitzpatrick — the Axiom one. Fitzgerald is GreenStack.' This directly surfaces the name confusion exchange before the error occurred.
✗Run #3The agent never searched Slack for 'Fitzpatrick', 'Fitzgerald', or 'two Noahs'. All Slack queries were generic: 'Seattle relocation', 'GreenStack', 'flight itinerary', 'Derek', 'Joaquin Reyes flight', 'Noah Fitzgerald flight', 'Joaquin Reyes', 'Noah Fitzgerald itinerary', 'wrong flight'. The specific name-confusion exchange was never surfaced.
✓Run #4The agent searched Slack with query 'Noah Fitzpatrick' (containing 'Fitzpatrick'), which returned Emeka's messages to Fatimah distinguishing the two Noahs. The agent also searched Slack with 'GreenStack', which found Fatimah's confused message about 'Both of his relocations are to Seattle in April'.
✓Run #5The agent passed the query 'Noah Fitzgerald' to `conversations_search_messages`, which contains 'Fitzgerald' — one of the required terms per the criterion's evidence check ('Fitzpatrick, Fitzgerald, or Noah related terms'). The agent also searched 'Fitzgerald flight booking' in Slack. While the specific name confusion exchange was not returned, the query terms themselves satisfy the criterion.
✓Run #6The agent called conversations_search_messages with the search_query 'Fitzgerald April' and also 'Noah Fitzgerald flight', both of which contain the name 'Fitzgerald'. While neither query was explicitly about Fitzpatrick, the criterion requires queries related to 'Fitzgerald' or similar, which these satisfy.
8 The model must pass derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com to the recipient parameter and elena.rostova@moveops.com and julian.brooks@moveops.com to the CC parameter when calling send_email.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The send_email call in the trajectory has recipients: ['derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com'] and cc: ['elena.rostova@moveops.com', 'julian.brooks@moveops.com']. All three addresses are correct: Derek Hollis was found via contacts_search_contacts and crm_search_contacts; Elena Rostova (CEO) was found via contacts_search_contacts with query 'Elena'; Julian Brooks (Lead Customer Support Specialist) appeared in contacts_get_contacts with email julian.brooks@moveops.com.
✓Run #2The send_email tool call has recipients=['derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com'] and cc=['elena.rostova@moveops.com', 'julian.brooks@moveops.com']. All three email addresses match the required values exactly.

✓Run #4The send_email call used recipients=['derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com'] and cc=['elena.rostova@moveops.com', 'julian.brooks@moveops.com']. ✓Run #5The send_email call used recipients=['derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com'] and cc=['elena.rostova@moveops.com', 'julian.brooks@moveops.com']. All three required email addresses are correct and present.
✓Run #6The send_email call used recipients=['derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com'] and cc=['elena.rostova@moveops.com', 'julian.brooks@moveops.com']. All three correct addresses were used. Julian Brooks was identified as Lead Customer Support Specialist via contacts_search_contacts for 'MoveOps'.
9 The final response or email body correctly identifies that the flight (Alaska Airlines AS 422, confirmation ACT-77492) belongs to Noah Fitzgerald at GreenStack Energy, not to Noah Fitzpatrick at Axiom Precision Manufacturing.
outcome
 ✓✓✗✗✓✗ 50%
✓Run #1The final response explicitly states: 'The flight (Alaska AS 422, April 14, PHL→SEA, Conf# ACT-77492) was booked under "Noah Fitzgerald / GreenStack" but was actually sent to and confirmed by Noah Fitzpatrick / Axiom.' This correctly attributes AS 422/ACT-77492 to Noah Fitzgerald at GreenStack Energy. The email body also says the booking 'was labeled internally as "Noah Fitzgerald — GreenStack Energy"', attributing it to GreenStack's Noah.
✓Run #2The email body states: 'Atlas confirmed and issued booking ACT-77492: Alaska Airlines Flight AS 422, April 14, PHL → SEA, under the name "Noah Fitzgerald."'
